steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists two events as 'important events in US civil rights _____ former establishing a Latino rights advocacy group and the latter legally affirming the rights of Latino students.' The words 'the former' and 'the latter' begin an absolute phrase that explains the two events. An absolute phrase modifying the full preceding clause needs a comma to attach to it — not a period, semicolon, or subordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — history the: No punctuation between the main clause and the explanatory phrase creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — history, the: A comma after 'history' correctly attaches the absolute phrase 'the former establishing... the latter legally affirming...' to the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — history, as the: Adding 'as' makes the phrase a subordinate clause — but 'the former establishing' is not a finite verb construction, so this is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — history. The: A period creates two sentences, but 'the former establishing... the latter legally affirming...' is not a complete sentence — it lacks a main verb. ✗
That is the correct answer.
